extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Adam caught NAVARRO Daniel in final meters after late attack, with THOMAS Geraint conceding less than 20 seconds to seal overall victory.
tactical_patterns: YATES Adam deliberately waited after initiating earlier on previous stages, suggesting learned patience pays on final summit finishes.
Sky protected BARDET Romain podium early, then abandoned THOMAS Geraint briefly on descent showing confidence in individual time gap.
BARDET Romain attacked on Col d'Amel with THOMAS and YATES responding, revealing podium contenders' hierarchy before final climb.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Learned to suppress early attacking instinct, held fire until final meters showing tactical maturation for Tour de France
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Absorbed attacks and limited losses under 20 seconds despite cracking late, confirming GC resilience under sustained pressure
rider_signals.NAVARRO Daniel: neutral: Front-running from early break nearly succeeded until final meters, demonstrating long-range breakaway viability even on hard mountain stages
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Attacking on penultimate climb failed to distance THOMAS or YATES, suggesting insufficient punch against in-form Sky riders
rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: Young rider showed strength reaching final climb first from early break despite facing multiple Cat 1 climbs
